subject: Databases
title: Optimistic vs Pessimistic Locking
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Optimistic vs Pessimistic Locking.

hook.text: Pessimistic locking grabs a lock upfront: 'Nobody touch this row.' Optimistic locking checks at save time.

buildup.text: Pessimistic: SELECT FOR UPDATE locks the row immediately. Safe but blocks others.

discovery.text: Optimistic: add a version column. Read version=3, update WHERE version=3 SET version=4. If it changes, retry.

twist.text: Optimistic locking works best when conflicts are rare. If conflicts are frequent, retries pile up.

climax.text: Web apps favor optimistic locking. Users edit forms offline and submit. Conflicts are resolved at save time.

punchline.text: Low conflict? Optimistic. High conflict? Pessimistic. Match to contention.

quiz.question: How does optimistic locking detect conflicts?
options: It checks a version column at update time and fails if the version has changed since the read
It locks the entire table before any read
It uses timestamps that are always synchronized across servers
